title: podman on Ubuntu 23.04 repeatedly downlods part of a container and filles disk
body:
### Issue Description

When pulling  nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:21.09-py3 with podman on Ubuntu VERSION="23.04 (Lunar Lobster)" the blobs are continually downloaded until the disk becomes full. Smaller imaged are pulled down as expected.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.podman pull nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:21.09-py3
2. wait
3.


### Describe the results you received

Stops with disk full error.

### Describe the results you expected

Downloads stops at actual image size.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
The problem is you are using the vfs storage driver, which is not very disk space friendly.  Should be using overlay.

  graphDriverName: vfs

Modify /etc/containers/storage.conf and then do a `podman system reset`

Dan,

There was no default storage.conf file in the deb installation file.
I copied the one from one of my RHEL-9 VMs.
Thanks for your prompt and helpful response.

Jeff

On 2023-11-02 17:10, Daniel J Walsh wrote:
> The problem is you are using the vfs storage driver, which is not very
> disk space friendly. Should be using overlay.
>
